title: forestiera segregata subsp. pinetorum
intro: Forestiera segregata subsp. pinetorum, also known as Mexican elderberry or pineland boxelder, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, particularly its berries and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The berries and roots can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
